item.title: Best Paper Runner-Up at CVPR 2021 Workshop
item.description:
Congratulations to Julian True! His paper “Motion Vector Extrapolation for Video Object Detection” won the Best Paper Runner-Up Award at the CVPR 2021 Robust Video Understanding Workshop.

The paper demonstrates that leveraging motion vectors from compressed video can decrease object detection latency by up to 25× while maintaining detection accuracy — a significant advance for real-time video understanding on resource-constrained devices.

item.title: Best Paper Award at IEEE ISM 2017
item.description:
Congratulations to Randy Tan! His paper on real-time human activity analysis won the Best Paper Award at the IEEE International Symposium on Multimedia (ISM) 2017, Workshop on Quality of Experience in Visual Media Processing and Transmission.

The work demonstrates a real-time system for recognizing and analyzing human activities from video, with applications in surveillance, healthcare monitoring, and interactive media.
